steps.title: Recognize the vertex form structure
steps.content:
The maximum rate of change occurs at population 8,000 with value 0.300. For a quadratic model, the vertex form $r(x) = a(x - h)^2 + k$ directly encodes the vertex $(h, k)$. This is much faster than standard form, which requires solving a system of equations.

With vertex $(8000, 0.300)$:
$$r(x) = a(x - 8000)^2 + 0.300$$

steps.title: Find the leading coefficient using the point (0, 0)
steps.content:
Substitute the point where population = 0 and rate of change = 0:
$$0 = a(0 - 8000)^2 + 0.300$$
$$0 = a(8000)^2 + 0.300$$
$$a = -\frac{0.300}{(8000)^2}$$
The negative coefficient confirms the parabola opens downward, with its maximum at the vertex.

steps.title: Evaluate at population 800 using the distance ratio
